unit_under_test.name: indexUsersById
unit_under_test.summary: Creates a lookup object from a list of users.

assumptions.statement: User IDs are unique within the input list.
assumptions.evidence: The implementation stores users in an object by id, which overwrites duplicate keys.
assumptions.risk: Duplicate IDs can silently drop earlier users and produce incorrect lookup results.
assumptions.test_idea: Pass two users with the same id and assert explicit duplicate handling.

assumptions.evidence: The id is used directly as an object key without validation.
assumptions.risk: Empty IDs create ambiguous lookup behavior.
assumptions.test_idea: Pass an empty id and assert explicit rejection.

test_plan.name: does_not_silently_overwrite_duplicate_user_ids
test_plan.setup: Create two users with the same id and different emails.
test_plan.assertion: The function throws or returns an explicit duplicate-id error.
test_plan.failure_mode: The implementation silently keeps only the second user.

test_plan.name: rejects_empty_user_ids
test_plan.setup: Create a user with an empty id.
test_plan.assertion: The function throws or returns an explicit invalid-id error.
test_plan.failure_mode: The implementation creates an empty-string lookup key.

gaps: Product behavior must decide whether duplicate IDs are rejected, merged, or preserved as a list.
